formatted_summary:
agent1 (Round 2 summarized): Mitochondria do not play a direct role in remodelling lace plant leaves during programmed cell death; unlike in animals, plants use hormone-driven growth variations and cellular changes without invoking mitocondrial-mediated apoptosis. This distinction highlights fundamental mechanistic divergence between animal and plant programs for controlled cell demise. \boxed{B}

The provided response contains multiple redundant summaries and repeated justifications. Please extract only one concise summary adhering strictly to the two-sentence requirement while preserving all essential clinical/technical content related directly to the query.

Mitochondria do not contribute to the remodelling of lace plant leaves during programmed cell death because plant cells employ hormonally regulated growth differences—such as auxin gradients—and structural changes instead of mitochondrial-dependent apoptosis seen in animals. These findings indicate that the molecular machinery governing programmatic cell death differs significantly between kingdoms, making the cited process irrelevant to plant leaf development.  
\boxed{B}. Answer choice: B
